title: Topological naturality: degree-1 restriction to the transverse preimage and $f_*h_{3,R}=h_{3,S}$, $g_{1,*}h_{3,S}=[S']$

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Two topological steps are asserted without full proof in the main argument:

(a) **Degree of the restriction.** "Since $f:(R,\partial R)\to(S,\partial S)$ is degree 1, it follows that $f:(f^{-1}(\Sigma_{a,b}),\partial)\to(\Sigma_{a,b},\partial)$ is also degree 1, and so $f_*(h_{2,R})=h_{2,S}$." This degree-of-transverse-restriction statement is a genuine intersection-theory lemma (it follows from naturality of the cap/intersection product with the relative fundamental class for $f$ transverse to $\Sigma_{a,b}$) and must be proved, not merely asserted.

(b) **Upgrade to 3-classes.** $f_*h_{3,R}=h_{3,S}$ is deduced from $f_*h_{2,R}=h_{2,S}$ via the connecting map $\partial:H_3(S,\partial S\cup\Sigma_{a,b})\to H_2(\partial S\cup\Sigma_{a,b})$ being an isomorphism. The isomorphism claim (from the long exact sequence of the triple, using $H_3$ and $H_2$ computations $\cong\ZZ$) should be verified.

(c) **Degree of $g_1$.** "$g_{1,*}(h_{3,S})$ is the fundamental homology class of $(S',\partial S')$." This requires computing the degree of the explicit map $g_1(y)=(y_1,y_2,r(y_3,y_4))$ with $r(y_3,y_4)=\min(10(|y_3-a|+|y_4-b|),S_3)$, i.e. checking it collapses the $(y_3,y_4)$-rectangle onto $[0,S_3]$ with degree $1$.

All three are F3-type unjustified key steps (standard but load-bearing).
